name: instant-canvas
description: Local canvas runtime for coding agents: renders canvas JSON (markdown, KPIs, tables, 26 chart kinds incl. 3D and ML plots, forms) and plain .md files as-is, prints them to PDF, and collects input/secrets straight to local files.

theme.description: Workspace default theme — the colors every document falls back to when it declares none of its own. A canvas (or a markdown file's companion canvas) always outranks it.

schema.description: A theme: a named preset, plus any color token override.

preset.description: Named starting point — supplies an accent, a chart colorway, and (for the dark presets and a few light ones) the paper itself.

palette.description: The chart colorway, 1–8 hex colors. ONE color is a lead the preset fills out; TWO or more ARE the colorway, exactly as given.

accent.description: The "accent" color token. Strict hex (#rgb or #rrggbb) — these values are assigned into live CSS, which would happily accept anything.

paper.description: The "paper" color token. Strict hex (#rgb or #rrggbb) — these values are assigned into live CSS, which would happily accept anything.

surface.description: The "surface" color token. Strict hex (#rgb or #rrggbb) — these values are assigned into live CSS, which would happily accept anything.

text.description: The "text" color token. Strict hex (#rgb or #rrggbb) — these values are assigned into live CSS, which would happily accept anything.

muted.description: The "muted" color token. Strict hex (#rgb or #rrggbb) — these values are assigned into live CSS, which would happily accept anything.

border.description: The "border" color token. Strict hex (#rgb or #rrggbb) — these values are assigned into live CSS, which would happily accept anything.

link.description: The "link" color token. Strict hex (#rgb or #rrggbb) — these values are assigned into live CSS, which would happily accept anything.

palettes.description: Named palette library, authored by the app's palette editor. Each entry is a theme object, offered in the browser beside the built-in presets. A LIBRARY, not new preset names: applying one copies its colors into the document.

schema.description: A map of palette name → theme object.

additionalProperties.description: One saved palette: a theme object.

preset.description: Named starting point — supplies an accent, a chart colorway, and (for the dark presets and a few light ones) the paper itself.

palette.description: The chart colorway, 1–8 hex colors. ONE color is a lead the preset fills out; TWO or more ARE the colorway, exactly as given.

accent.description: The "accent" color token. Strict hex (#rgb or #rrggbb) — these values are assigned into live CSS, which would happily accept anything.

paper.description: The "paper" color token. Strict hex (#rgb or #rrggbb) — these values are assigned into live CSS, which would happily accept anything.

surface.description: The "surface" color token. Strict hex (#rgb or #rrggbb) — these values are assigned into live CSS, which would happily accept anything.

border.description: The "border" color token. Strict hex (#rgb or #rrggbb) — these values are assigned into live CSS, which would happily accept anything.

link.description: The "link" color token. Strict hex (#rgb or #rrggbb) — these values are assigned into live CSS, which would happily accept anything.

node.description: Node.js >= 20 with npx — fetches and runs the instant-canvas CLI and its per-workspace kernel (the CLI itself has zero npm dependencies).
